evidence: 34 test files, 278 @Test methods covering all major packages: api, config, exploration, indexer, query, utils, viz
StubQueryExecutor base class in src/test/ with override-friendly methods — anonymous subclasses used throughout to provide canned data per test
TestExchangeFactory.MockExchange provides a reusable fake HttpExchange for all handler tests
Happy-path AND validation/400-error paths tested for every API handler (ExplorationHandlersTest, ConcordanceHandlersTest, SketchHandlersTest, etc.)
5 test classes marked @Disabled because they require a live BlackLab index — all clearly documented with env var instructions (CONCEPT_SKETCH_TEST_INDEX)
All integration-gated tests additionally use Assumptions.assumeTrue so they skip gracefully rather than fail if env var is absent
ExplorationService interface is never used as the declared type in any test — all tests inject SemanticFieldExplorer directly, tightly coupling tests to the concrete class rather than the abstraction
BcqlPatternUtils static utility methods are tested inside BlackLabSnippetParserTest rather than a dedicated file — discoverable but misnamed for navigation
No runnable integration tests across module boundaries exist in CI — all cross-module paths (indexer → executor → explorer → handler) are only exercised end-to-end with a live index

strengths: Extensible StubQueryExecutor base class enables concise, targeted anonymous overrides in every unit test without test-specific mocking frameworks
API handler tests cover both success (200) and validation-failure (400) paths for every handler, including multi-parameter and cross-field validations
Integration tests requiring a live index are properly isolated with @Disabled + Assumptions.assumeTrue, so they fail neither in CI nor silently skip
Core business logic classes (SemanticFieldExplorer, MultiSeedExplorer, CollocateQueryHelper, CollocateProfileComparator, BcqlPatternUtils) all have dedicated unit test coverage
Model-layer records and trivial constants are correctly left untested — the strategy correctly avoids low-value test noise
test_strategy.issue_character: The unit test layer is solid and consistent; the strategic gap is that all cross-module integration paths are disabled and can only be run in specific environments, leaving the full request pipeline (handler → explorer → executor → index) unverifiable in CI.
test_strategy.score_rationale: The codebase sits solidly in the 80-range: a developer making changes to the unit-tested core (exploration logic, CQL patterns, API validation) will have good confidence. However, there is a persistent blind spot — no integration test runs unconditionally in CI, meaning regressions in the BlackLabQueryExecutor or indexer pipelines can only be caught manually. Tests are also coupled to SemanticFieldExplorer as a concrete class rather than the ExplorationService interface, which weakens contract enforcement. These gaps push the score from ~85 down to ~79: the strategy is good but a developer working on the corpus-query layer has no automated safety net.

issues.summary: All integration tests that touch BlackLabQueryExecutor or the indexer are @Disabled — no CI-runnable cross-module path exists
evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutorIntegrationTest is annotated @Disabled with 'Requires a live BlackLab index'
DependencySketchTest is annotated @Disabled with same rationale
BlackLabConllUIndexerTest is annotated @Disabled because conllu-sentences.blf.yaml is not distributed with source
BlackLabQueryExecutorTest's LiveIndex nested class is also @Disabled
No alternative lightweight integration test (e.g. an in-memory Lucene index) exists to replace these
issues.suggestion: Create a small self-contained integration test module (or test profile) that builds a minimal BlackLab in-memory or TempDir index from a 3–5 sentence CoNLL-U fixture, then exercises BlackLabQueryExecutor.executeCollocations and executeSurfacePattern end-to-end. This can run in CI without the production corpus, providing a runnable cross-module safety net. Alternatively, mark the existing disabled tests as a manual-only profile and document clearly in the README that CI coverage stops at the stub layer.

issues.summary: Tests inject SemanticFieldExplorer directly rather than through ExplorationService, bypassing contract enforcement
evidence: ExplorationHandlersTest creates 'new SemanticFieldExplorer(emptyExecutor(), config)' and passes it directly to handlers, not as ExplorationService
SemanticFieldExplorerTest declares its test class against SemanticFieldExplorer, not ExplorationService
ExplorationHandlers constructor accepts ExplorationService but tests never exercise this as the declared type — a second implementation would need new tests from scratch
No test exercises the compareCollocateProfiles or exploreMultiSeed contract via the ExplorationService interface type
issues.suggestion: Declare test variables as 'ExplorationService explorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(...)' instead of 'SemanticFieldExplorer explorer = ...'. This is a one-line change per test that costs nothing but will catch any future contract divergence between implementations.

root_causes: The project depends on a large external corpus index (74M sentences) which cannot be bundled with the source — this is the root cause of all @Disabled integration tests and is an inherent operational constraint
Tests were written after the code and focused on validating correctness of already-built units rather than driving design through the interface layer, leading to concrete-class coupling
